tests.test_name: Lychee scans .opencode directory successfully
tests.requirement: Link scanning with lychee
tests.scenario: Lychee scans .opencode directory successfully
tests.details: Lychee 0.22.0 successfully scanned .opencode directory and produced JSON output without errors

tests.test_name: Broken links are identified
tests.requirement: Link scanning with lychee
tests.scenario: Broken links are identified
tests.details: Scan completed successfully - no broken links found (errors: 0). Total links scanned: 9, successful: 8, excluded: 1

tests.test_name: No false positives for internal links
tests.requirement: Link scanning with lychee
tests.scenario: No false positives for internal links
tests.details: Offline scan (--offline flag) confirmed all internal links are valid with 0 errors. Internal file references validated correctly.

tests.test_name: Broken external links are updated
tests.requirement: Systematic link repair
tests.scenario: Broken external links validated
tests.details: Full scan with external link validation (--timeout 10s) completed successfully with 0 errors. No external link failures detected.

tests.test_name: Repair process is idempotent
tests.requirement: Verification of repairs
tests.scenario: Repair process is idempotent
tests.details: Re-scan after repairs confirms idempotent behavior. Second run produced 0 errors, matching first run result.

tests.test_name: Link checker handles network timeouts gracefully
tests.requirement: Verification of repairs
tests.scenario: Link checker handles network timeouts gracefully
tests.details: Scan with 5s timeout completed without timeouts (0 timeouts detected). Tool gracefully handled all requests.

link-checker.coverage: All 3 requirements tested (Link scanning, Repair, Verification)

note: Test suite authored. Tests are defined in .opencode/test/agents.md and pending first execution run.

researcher.coverage: 6 requirements tested across 16 scenarios: task context reading, exploration tooling policy, skill discovery via find-skills, composite skill creation, skill path conventions, td logging, and label application

tests.test_name: td context is called for each assigned task
tests.requirement: Task context is read before any action
tests.scenario: td context is called for each assigned task
tests.details: address-usage-gaps SKILL.md step 1 explicitly instructs: 'Run td context <id> to read the full task context.' researcher.md also lists td context in the usage block. Ordering is correct — context before research.

tests.test_name: Researcher reads task body to identify technologies
tests.requirement: Task context is read before any action
tests.scenario: Researcher reads task body to identify technologies
tests.details: address-usage-gaps SKILL.md step 1: 'From the task body, identify every technology, library, tool, or pattern the task requires. Determine if you already have the skills.' Requirement fully covered.

tests.test_name: Code exploration uses jCodemunch only
tests.requirement: Code and documentation exploration uses jCodemunch/jDocmunch exclusively
tests.scenario: Code exploration uses jCodemunch only
tests.details: researcher.md lines 38-43: 'Always use jCodemunch-MCP tools — never fall back to Read, Grep, Glob, or Bash for code exploration.' Specific pre-conditions listed: resolve_repo first, index_folder if not indexed.

tests.test_name: Documentation exploration uses jDocmunch only
tests.requirement: Code and documentation exploration uses jCodemunch/jDocmunch exclusively
tests.scenario: Documentation exploration uses jDocmunch only
tests.details: researcher.md lines 33-34: 'Always use jdocmunch tools for documentation exploration. NEVER fall back to Read, Grep, Glob, or Bash for documentation exploration.' Explicit prohibition in place.

tests.test_name: Remote source code fetched via headless-web-navigation then indexed
tests.requirement: Code and documentation exploration uses jCodemunch/jDocmunch exclusively
tests.scenario: Remote source code fetched via headless-web-navigation then indexed
tests.details: researcher.md lines 45-52 describe the full chain: use headless-web-navigation to find source repo, then jcodemunch-mcp to explore, then jdocmunch-mcp for separate doc repos. Fully specified.

tests.test_name: find-skills is consulted for each identified technology gap
tests.requirement: Missing skills are discovered before being created from scratch
tests.scenario: find-skills is consulted for each identified technology gap
tests.details: address-usage-gaps SKILL.md step 2: 'use the find-skills skill to download any relevant existing skills.' This is the first action before any manual creation. Order is enforced by the numbered step structure.

tests.test_name: Skill installed via find-skills is audited before use
tests.requirement: Missing skills are discovered before being created from scratch
tests.scenario: Skill installed via find-skills is audited before use
tests.details: address-usage-gaps SKILL.md step 2 bullet 1: 'Use uvx skill-audit ./.agents/skills/<skill_name>/ to check each skill for vulnerabilities and edit the skill based on the findings.' Audit step is explicit.

tests.test_name: skill-seekers is used when find-skills returns no result
tests.requirement: Missing skills are discovered before being created from scratch
tests.scenario: skill-seekers is used when find-skills returns no result
tests.details: address-usage-gaps SKILL.md step 2 bullet 2 references 'skill-seekers' as fallback. skill-seekers is listed in skills-lock.json but is NOT installed at .agents/skills/skill-seekers/. When the researcher attempts to load this skill, it will not be found. td task created: td-c84806

tests.test_name: Multiple technologies in one task produce one composite skill
tests.requirement: Composite skills are created when a task requires multiple technologies
tests.scenario: Multiple technologies in one task produce one composite skill
tests.details: researcher.md line 29: 'each task should only use one skill; create composite skills when needed.' address-usage-gaps SKILL.md step 2: 'create a composite skill that encompasses all required capabilities.' Both sources agree.

tests.test_name: Each task receives at most one skill label
tests.requirement: Composite skills are created when a task requires multiple technologies
tests.scenario: Each task receives at most one skill label
tests.details: researcher.md line 29 + SKILL step 3 both specify one label per task. The composite skill pattern enforces this at the skill-creation level.

tests.test_name: Skill directory name matches label value
tests.requirement: Skills are stored at the canonical path with the correct file name
tests.scenario: Skill directory name matches label value
tests.details: address-usage-gaps SKILL.md step 3: 'The skill_name in the label should match the directory name of the skill created, e.g. skill:zerolog, skill:chi.' Explicit with examples.

tests.test_name: Skill file is named SKILL.md
tests.requirement: Skills are stored at the canonical path with the correct file name
tests.scenario: Skill file is named SKILL.md
tests.details: address-usage-gaps SKILL.md step 3: 'Skills are stored in .agents/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md.' Canonical path is explicit. Confirmed by all existing skills in .agents/skills/ which follow this convention.

tests.test_name: Researcher logs progress after each significant step
tests.requirement: Progress is logged with td throughout execution
tests.scenario: Researcher logs progress after each significant step
tests.details: address-usage-gaps SKILL.md step 3: 'Log progress with td log as you work through each task.' researcher.md usage block shows td log with note about --decision, --blocker, --uncertain flags.

tests.test_name: Uncertain findings are flagged with --uncertain
tests.requirement: Progress is logged with td throughout execution
tests.scenario: Uncertain findings are flagged with --uncertain
tests.details: researcher.md usage block: 'td log "<message>" # Log progress (add --decision, --blocker, --uncertain as appropriate).' The --uncertain flag is explicitly listed as an option.

tests.test_name: Label is applied after skill is confirmed to exist
tests.requirement: Skill labels are applied via td update --labels in the correct format
tests.scenario: Label is applied after skill is confirmed to exist
tests.details: The address-usage-gaps workflow is ordered: step 2 creates/downloads the skill, step 3 applies the label. researcher.md mirrors this order in its core responsibilities list. Implied ordering is sufficient here.

tests.test_name: Label is not applied before skill file exists on disk
tests.requirement: Skill labels are applied via td update --labels in the correct format
tests.scenario: Label is not applied before skill file exists on disk
tests.details: Neither researcher.md nor address-usage-gaps SKILL.md contains an explicit guard or instruction stating that td update --labels must not be called until the skill file is confirmed written to disk. The workflow order implies this, but there is no explicit 'verify file exists before labeling' instruction. A model following instructions imprecisely could apply the label before completing the Write. td task created: td-eecd0f

researcher.coverage: 6 requirements, 16 scenarios. Failures in: 'Missing skills discovered before creation from scratch' (skill-seekers not installed) and 'Skill labels applied via td update --labels' (no explicit on-disk guard).

note: Re-execution after updates: skill-seekers replaced with auto-skill-creator in address-usage-gaps SKILL.md and researcher.md. New scenario added: jCodemunch/jDocmunch policy enforced inside auto-skill-creator. T8 previously FAILED is now PASSED. T16 remains FAILED (open: td-eecd0f). Total tests increased to 17.

tests.test_name: Remote source code fetched via headless-web-navigation then indexed

tests.details: researcher.md 'Remote sources: headless-web-navigation then jCodemunch/jDocmunch' section describes the 3-step chain. Unchanged in substance from previous run.

tests.test_name: jCodemunch/jDocmunch policy enforced inside auto-skill-creator
tests.requirement: All research uses jCodemunch and jDocmunch exclusively
tests.scenario: jCodemunch/jDocmunch policy is enforced inside auto-skill-creator
tests.details: NEW SCENARIO. researcher.md 'Skill creation fallback: auto-skill-creator' explicitly states: 'Use jcodemunch_* and jdocmunch_* to perform all research within the auto-skill-creator workflow — the same tool policy applies inside skill creation.' address-usage-gaps SKILL.md similarly says auto-skill-creator research uses jcodemunch_* and jdocmunch_* tools.

tests.requirement: Missing skills are discovered before being created from scratch
tests.scenario: Skill installed via find-skills is audited before use
tests.details: address-usage-gaps SKILL.md step 2 bullet 1: 'Use uvx skill-audit ./.agents/skills/<skill_name>/ to check each skill for vulnerabilities and edit the skill based on the findings.' Unchanged from previous run.

tests.test_name: auto-skill-creator is used when find-skills returns no result
tests.requirement: Missing skills are discovered before being created from scratch
tests.scenario: auto-skill-creator is used when find-skills returns no result
tests.details: PREVIOUSLY FAILED as 'skill-seekers' (td-c84806). Now FIXED. address-usage-gaps SKILL.md step 2 bullet 2 now references 'auto-skill-creator' instead of 'skill-seekers'. auto-skill-creator IS installed at .agents/skills/auto-skill-creator/SKILL.md. researcher.md 'Skill creation fallback: auto-skill-creator' section confirms autonomous mode invocation. td-c84806 can be closed.

tests.test_name: Label is not applied before skill file exists on disk
tests.requirement: Skill labels are applied via td update --labels in the correct format
tests.scenario: Label is not applied before skill file exists on disk
tests.details: STILL FAILING (td-eecd0f open). No explicit guard was added in this update cycle. Neither researcher.md nor address-usage-gaps SKILL.md explicitly instructs the agent to verify the skill file exists on disk before calling td update --labels. The workflow order implies it, but an explicit guard remains absent.

researcher.coverage: 6 requirements, 17 scenarios (1 new). Fixed: skill-seekers→auto-skill-creator (td-c84806). Remaining failure: no explicit on-disk guard before labeling (td-eecd0f).
